Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 242 upvotes, 25 comments
  2. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 161 upvotes, 29 comments
  3. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide 328 upvotes, 76 comments
  4. Building a new Flash 519 upvotes, 149 comments
  5. Nvidia PersonaPlex 7B on Apple Silicon: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech in Swift 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 256 upvotes, 77 comments
  7. “It turns out” (2010) 278 upvotes, 87 comments
  8. The View from RSS 111 upvotes, 30 comments
  9. Show HN: Poppy – a simple app to stay intentional with relationships 76 upvotes, 19 comments
  10. Google Workspace CLI 523 upvotes, 185 comments
  11. You Just Reveived 123 upvotes, 36 comments
  12. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 647 upvotes, 290 comments
  13. Chaos and Dystopian news for the dead internet survivors 92 upvotes, 33 comments
  14. Picking Up a Zillion Pieces of Litter 107 upvotes, 40 comments
  15. Smalltalk's Browser: Unbeatable, yet Not Enough 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying 74 upvotes, 27 comments
  17. Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic 144 upvotes, 63 comments
  18. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 114 upvotes, 50 comments
  19. Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’ 524 upvotes, 290 comments
  20. Dbslice: Extract a slice of your production database to reproduce bugs 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. Glaze by Raycast 217 upvotes, 130 comments
  22. Dulce et Decorum Est (1921) 118 upvotes, 65 comments
  23. Relax NG is a schema language for XML 17 upvotes, 5 comments
  24. Malm Whale 26 upvotes, 10 comments
  25. The Self-Help Trap: What 20 Years of "Optimizing" Has Taught Me 27 upvotes, 12 comments
  26. What Python's asyncio primitives get wrong about shared state 46 upvotes, 25 comments
  27. NRC issues first commercial reactor construction approval in 10 years [pdf] 104 upvotes, 69 comments
  28. AMD will bring its "Ryzen AI" processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 53 upvotes, 42 comments
  29. MacBook Neo 1757 upvotes, 2046 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments
